articles.title: اليوم.. عرض فيلم “ملكة القطن” بحضور أبطاله بنادى السينما الأفريقية
articles.summary: يعرض نادي السينما الأفريقية، الذي يقيمه مهرجان الأقصر للسينما الأفريقية بالتعاون مع صندوق التنمية الثقافية، الفيلم السوداني “ملكة القطن”، وذلك اليوم السبت 4 يوليو، في تمام الساعة السابعة مساءً، بسينما الهناجر بدار الأوبرا المصرية، ويعقب العرض ندوة لمناقشة الفيلم مع الجمهور والنقاد بحضور أبطال الفيلم الفنانة السودانية حرم بشير، وبطلة الفيلم مهاد مرتضي، والفنانين السودانيين …
articles.context: The screening of "ملكة القطن" (Queen of Cotton) comes at a time when Sudan is grappling with significant political turmoil following the military coup in October 2021 and ongoing conflicts, particularly in Darfur. The film, which explores themes of identity and resilience, serves as a cultural response to the challenges facing Sudanese society. Additionally, the collaboration between the African Cinema Club and the Luxor African Film Festival highlights efforts to promote Sudanese cinema amidst these turbulent times.

articles.title: كيفية حجز اسم مستخدم على واتساب.. خطوات بسطية
articles.summary: أعلنت شركة ميتا في وقت سابق من هذا الأسبوع أن منصة المراسلة الشهيرة واتساب ستُضيف ميزة أسماء المستخدمين. تُتيح هذه الأسماء استبدال أرقام الهواتف، مما يسمح للمستخدمين بالحفاظ على خصوصية معلومات الاتصال الشخصية. لم تُفعّل ميزة أسماء المستخدمين بعد، وسيتم إطلاقها عالميًا خلال الأشهر القادمة. ومع ذلك، فُتح باب حجز أسماء المستخدمين، وقد سارع المستخدمون …
articles.context: تأتي ميزة أسماء المستخدمين على واتساب في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث يعاني البلد من أزمة سياسية وأمنية متفاقمة منذ الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019. مع تصاعد التوترات بين القوات المسلحة والدعم السريع، يسعى المواطنون إلى حماية خصوصيتهم في ظل مخاوف من المراقبة والاعتقالات. هذه الميزة الجديدة قد توفر وسيلة للتواصل أكثر أمانًا في بيئة تتسم بعدم الاستقرار.

articles.title: ماسك يفرض سقفا أسبوعيا لإنفاق موظفى “تسلا” على أدوات الذكاء الاصطناعى

articles.context: تأتي خطوة إيلون ماسك في فرض سقف إنفاق موظفي "تسلا" على أدوات الذكاء الاصطناعي في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات اقتصادية كبيرة، بما في ذلك ارتفاع معدلات التضخم والصراعات المستمرة. كما أن تطور التكنولوجيا في المنطقة، وخاصة في مجالات الذكاء الاصطناعي، قد يؤثر على مستقبل الاقتصاد السوداني، مما يجعل هذه القرارات ذات أهمية خاصة في سياق سعي البلاد نحو الابتكار والتنمية.

articles.title: الهند تحذر تليجرام من إجراءات قانونية بسبب قرصنة المحتوى
articles.summary: صعدت الحكومة الهندية من ضغوطها على تطبيق تليجرام، مطالبة المنصة باتخاذ إجراءات استباقية للحد من انتشار المحتوى المقرصن، مؤكدة أن الاكتفاء بحذف القنوات المخالفة بعد الإبلاغ عنها لم يعد كافيًا للوفاء بالالتزامات القانونية المفروضة على المنصات الرقمية. وأكدت وزارة الإعلام والإذاعة الهندية، في إخطار رسمي، أن نهج إزالة القنوات المخالفة بشكل فردي لا يثبت قيام …
articles.context: تأتي تحذيرات الحكومة الهندية لتطبيق تليجرام في وقت تتزايد فيه المخاوف بشأن حقوق الملكية الفكرية في ظل انتشار المحتوى المقرصن، خاصة بعد إصدار قانون تكنولوجيا المعلومات في الهند عام 2021. هذا القانون يفرض التزامات جديدة على المنصات الرقمية، مما يعكس الضغط المتزايد على الشركات التكنولوجية لضمان الامتثال. كما أن هذه الخطوة تأتي في ظل تصاعد التوترات بين الهند والدول التي تستضيف منصات التواصل الاجتماعي الكبرى، مثل روسيا.

articles.title: أطعمة شائعة تزيد من حدة أعراض الربو.. كيف تحافظ على صحة رئتيك؟
articles.summary: يُعد الربو من الأمراض التنفسية المزمنة التي تتطلب إدارة يومية دقيقة، ولا يقتصر ذلك على الالتزام بالأدوية فقط، بل يمتد أيضًا إلى نمط الحياة والنظام الغذائي، ورغم أن الطعام لا يُسبب الإصابة بالربو، إلا أن بعض الأطعمة قد تُحفز ظهور الأعراض أو تزيد من حدتها لدى بعض المرضى، في حين تساهم أطعمة أخرى في دعم …
articles.context: في السودان، يعاني العديد من السكان من مشاكل صحية نتيجة النزاعات المستمرة والأزمات الإنسانية، مما يزيد من أهمية إدارة الأمراض المزمنة مثل الربو. منذ اندلاع النزاع في 2019، تدهورت الظروف الصحية، مما يجعل الوعي بالنظام الغذائي وتأثيره على الصحة التنفسية أمرًا حيويًا. كما أن نقص الوصول إلى الرعاية الصحية يعزز الحاجة إلى استراتيجيات وقائية مثل تعديل النظام الغذائي لدعم صحة الرئتين.

articles.summary: يعيش النجم محمد إمام حالة من النشاط الفني اللافت خلال عام 2026، بعدما جمع بين السينما والدراما، إلى جانب استعداده لخوض أولى تجاربه على خشبة المسرح، في خطوة جديدة بمشواره الفني، ليواصل حضوره القوي بأكثر من عمل خلال العام. محمد إمام في سينما 2026 بفيلمين ويُعرض لمحمد إمام حاليًا في دور العرض السينمائية فيلم “صقر …

articles.context: تأتي تعاقدات الأندية الرياضية في السودان، مثل نادي النصر، في ظل تحديات اقتصادية وسياسية كبيرة، حيث يعاني البلد من أزمة اقتصادية خانقة منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023. يسعى النادي لتعزيز صفوفه وتحقيق الاستقرار في ظل الظروف الصعبة، مما يجعل تعيين مدرب جديد مثل أنج بوستيكوجلو خطوة مهمة نحو إعادة بناء الفريق وتحقيق النجاح في المنافسات المحلية والدولية.

articles.title: How funerals and weddings remind men of who they are yet to become
articles.summary: For a man, every loss is a reminder of his unfulfilled potential.
articles.context: In Kenya, the ongoing challenges of youth unemployment and societal expectations have intensified since the 2017 elections, where political divisions deepened. The recent rise in violent crime and the economic impact of the COVID-19 pandemic have exacerbated feelings of inadequacy among men, who often grapple with their roles in family and society. Funerals and weddings serve as poignant reminders of both loss and the aspirations that remain unfulfilled in this context.

articles.title: Highway crime queens: How women are being used to lure drivers into deadly robberies
articles.summary: The women are used to distract unsuspecting drivers before their colleagues strike.
articles.context: In recent years, Kenya has seen a surge in highway robberies, particularly along the Nairobi-Mombasa route, exacerbated by rising unemployment and economic instability following the COVID-19 pandemic. The 2022 elections heightened tensions, leading to increased lawlessness in some areas as police resources were diverted. Criminal gangs, including those using women as decoys, have exploited this environment, posing significant challenges to security forces and raising concerns about public safety and gender exploitation in crime.

articles.context: The incident involving the impersonation of a police officer comes amid heightened tensions in Sudan following the 2021 military coup that ousted Prime Minister Abdalla Hamdok. The ongoing power struggle between military leaders and civilian groups has led to widespread unrest and distrust in law enforcement. This particular case highlights the challenges of maintaining public order and the potential for misinformation to exacerbate the already volatile political climate in the country.

articles.title: No love lost for volleyball siblings Tata and Itoo in high-octane league semi-final clash
articles.summary: Volleyball sisters face off in KVF Women’s Division One League semi-final showdown.
articles.context: The KVF Women’s Division One League has gained prominence in Kenya's sports landscape, especially following the 2021 reforms aimed at enhancing women's participation in athletics. The rivalry between Tata and Itoo, both standout players from the same family, reflects broader themes of competition and empowerment in a country where women's sports have historically been underfunded. Their semi-final clash on March 15, 2023, symbolizes a significant moment for female athletes in Kenya, showcasing their growing visibility and impact.

articles.title: Gachagua: Why United Opposition will only name candidate next year
articles.summary: Former Deputy President says the Opposition is not in a hurry to name its joint presidential...
articles.context: The United Opposition in Kenya, formed after the 2022 elections, seeks to consolidate its power against the ruling coalition led by President William Ruto. The opposition's decision to delay naming a joint presidential candidate until 2024 reflects ongoing tensions following the controversial elections, where allegations of electoral malpractice were rampant. This strategic move aims to unify various factions, including Raila Odinga's Azimio coalition, ahead of the 2027 elections.

articles.title: Over 1,000 Sudanese voluntarily return home from Egypt
articles.summary: Committee for voluntary return organizes 24 buses carrying Sudanese citizens back home, according to Sudan state media
articles.context: The return of over 1,000 Sudanese citizens from Egypt comes amid ongoing instability following the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, primarily between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. The situation has led to a humanitarian crisis, prompting many to seek refuge in neighboring countries. The Sudanese government, under pressure to address the needs of its displaced citizens, has initiated organized repatriation efforts, reflecting a complex interplay of regional migration and domestic strife.

The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to stabilize the region, but implementation has been slow, leading to widespread food insecurity. The delay in cash assistance is particularly significant as it highlights the ongoing struggles of local communities to access essential support amid a backdrop of political instability and economic hardship.

articles.title: Woman beaten to death by partner: Delayed justice and why Kitale judge faulted police
articles.summary: Judgment paints a disturbing picture of domestic violence, delayed justice and failure by...
articles.context: In Kenya, domestic violence remains a pervasive issue, exacerbated by systemic failures in the judicial process. The 2010 Constitution aimed to enhance women's rights, yet enforcement remains weak, as highlighted by the 2021 case of a woman in Kitale who was brutally killed by her partner. Recent public outcry over delayed justice has intensified scrutiny on law enforcement and judicial accountability, particularly following a 2023 ruling that criticized police inaction in domestic violence cases.

This initiative aims to foster community resilience and support local development in a region still recovering from years of civil war, particularly as tensions remain between various ethnic groups and political factions, including the SPLM-IO and SPLM.

The region has seen a rise in youth involvement in drug abuse and violence, exacerbated by the lack of recreational activities. The recent ban on minors in discos, announced in October 2023, reflects community efforts to curb these issues and promote safer environments for youth amid ongoing concerns about public safety and child welfare.

articles.context: Moi University, established in 1984, has faced financial challenges and declining enrollment in recent years, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic. The Kenyan government’s 2021 directive to universities to streamline operations has intensified scrutiny on Moi University’s management. The recent backing of a six-point plan by MPs reflects ongoing efforts to revitalize the institution amidst a broader national discourse on higher education reform and funding, particularly in light of the 2022 Education Act amendments.

articles.title: The world knows how to stop violence against women. So, why isn't it working?
articles.summary: Almost 840 million women worldwide have experienced physical or sexual violence, a number that...
articles.context: In Kenya, the 2010 Constitution aimed to enhance women's rights and combat gender-based violence, yet implementation remains weak. The ongoing political instability, exacerbated by the contentious 2022 elections, has diverted attention from women's safety issues. Additionally, the COVID-19 pandemic led to a surge in domestic violence cases, highlighting the urgent need for effective enforcement of laws and support systems for survivors, as organizations like UN Women report alarming statistics on violence against women in the region.

articles.title: Health ministry assessment team completes visit to Northern Bahr el Ghazal
articles.summary: An assessment team from the national Ministry of Health deployed to Northern Bahr el Ghazal State has completed a five-day mission aimed at assessing the quality of healthcare services. articles.context: Northern Bahr el Ghazal has faced significant challenges in healthcare delivery due to ongoing instability and conflict since South Sudan's independence in 2011. The region has been affected by intercommunal violence and the broader civil war, which officially ended with the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan in 2018. This assessment by the Ministry of Health is crucial for addressing the healthcare gaps exacerbated by these historical and ongoing issues.

articles.context: South Sudan has faced significant challenges in governance and public service delivery since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by ongoing conflict and economic instability. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of Conflict in South Sudan aimed to restore peace, yet bureaucratic inefficiencies persist. The arrival of new passport booklets is crucial as it signals a potential improvement in civil services, which are vital for national identity and mobility amid a backdrop of political transition and humanitarian needs.

articles.title: Dennis Lusava walked into a police post...he was later found dead
articles.context: Dennis Lusava's death comes amid heightened tensions in Kenya following the controversial August 2022 elections, which saw President William Ruto's administration face accusations of human rights abuses. The police have been under scrutiny for their handling of protests and public dissent, particularly in regions like Kisumu, where police brutality has sparked outrage. This incident raises concerns about accountability and the safety of civilians in a politically charged environment.

articles.title: Empowering women could unlock Kenya’s dairy sector
articles.summary: The dairy industry benefits from women's labour yet denies them access to land and livestock.
articles.context: In Kenya, women contribute approximately 70% of the labor in the dairy sector, yet they face systemic barriers to land ownership and livestock access, rooted in colonial land policies and patriarchal traditions. The 2010 Constitution aimed to address gender inequality, but implementation remains weak. Recent initiatives, such as the 2021 National Dairy Policy, highlight the need for gender-responsive strategies to empower women, making this issue particularly urgent as the country seeks to boost agricultural productivity and economic resilience.

articles.title: Matiang’i' chance meeting with Junet, Arati sets tongues wagging
articles.summary: Matiang'i dismissed the speculation, describing the encounter as coincidental.
articles.context: The chance meeting between Kenyan Interior Cabinet Secretary Fred Matiang’i and opposition leaders Junet Mohamed and Arati comes amid heightened political tensions following the controversial 2022 general elections, which saw President William Ruto's administration face criticism over governance issues. The ongoing fallout from the elections, including protests led by the opposition coalition Azimio la Umoja, underscores the fragile political landscape, making any interaction between key figures noteworthy in the current climate of distrust and speculation.

articles.title: القطرية تطلق 3 رحلات أسبوعياً إلى بورتسودان

articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في ظل الأوضاع المتوترة في السودان، حيث يشهد البلد صراعات داخلية منذ اندلاع النزاع في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. يعكس إطلاق الخطوط الجوية القطرية لرحلاتها إلى بورتسودان رغبة قطر في تعزيز استثماراتها في المنطقة ودعم الاقتصاد السوداني، خاصة مع الحاجة الملحة لتوفير خدمات النقل الجوي في ظل الأزمات الحالية.

Ruto's administration has faced challenges, including economic hardships and ethnic tensions, which have prompted his allies to rally support for a second term. The mention of a 2032 succession plan reflects ongoing power struggles within the Kenya Kwanza coalition, particularly as Ruto navigates alliances with influential figures like Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua.

Ruben Loro Karlo was sworn […] The post Kiir tasks new Media Authority board with strengthening media sector appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The establishment of the new Media Authority board comes amid ongoing challenges in South Sudan's media landscape, characterized by government censorship and restrictions on press freedom. Following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which aimed to stabilize the country after years of civil war, the media's role in promoting transparency and accountability has become increasingly critical as the government seeks to rebuild trust with its citizens and the international community.

articles.title: Here is how social media can ruin your sex life
articles.summary: Always remember that there is a lot of unfiltered medical information online.
articles.context: In Kenya, the rise of social media has transformed how individuals access health information, often leading to the spread of misinformation. The 2020 COVID-19 pandemic exacerbated this trend, as many turned to online platforms for guidance on sexual health, resulting in confusion and anxiety. Additionally, the ongoing debate over the Sexual Offenses Act and its implications for sexual rights has intensified discussions around sexual health, making accurate information more crucial than ever.

articles.title: EHRCO Accuses Gov’t Forces of Abuses in South Ethiopia, Demands Accountability
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Human Rights Council (EHRCO) released a statement on July 2, 2026, accusing government armed forces of committing human rights violations against residents in the Zayse Woreda of the South Ethiopia Regional State. The rights organization stated that armed forces conducted attacks between June 18 and June 29, 2026, across ten kebeles in the […]
articles.context: The allegations come amid ongoing tensions in Ethiopia following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which exacerbated ethnic divisions and violence across the country. The federal government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has faced criticism for its handling of regional conflicts, particularly in the Southern Nations, Nationalities, and Peoples' Region. The EHRCO's report highlights the urgent need for accountability as the government grapples with increasing unrest and calls for justice from various ethnic groups.

articles.title: New Board to Oversee Electronic Invoice Accreditation as Tax Officials Sustain Digital Push
articles.summary: The Ministry of Revenues has established an Inspection and Accreditation board under the newly issued Electronic Invoice System Administration Directive No. 1142/2018, introducing a formal inspection and certification framework for electronic invoicing systems used by taxpayers, software developers, and digital marketplace operators. The new organization is mandated to inspect, accredit, and monitor electronic invoicing systems […]
articles.context: The establishment of the Inspection and Accreditation board comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing efforts to modernize its tax system and enhance revenue collection, particularly following the 2020 fiscal reforms aimed at addressing economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict. The new Electronic Invoice System Administration Directive, issued in 2018, reflects the government's commitment to digital transformation, crucial for improving transparency and efficiency in a country where informal markets have historically undermined tax compliance.

articles.title: The NGO on the Frontlines of Ethiopia’s Trachoma Eradication Efforts
articles.summary: Caused by repeated infections with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis, trachoma is the world’s leading infectious cause of blindness. It primarily affects impoverished rural communities with limited access to clean water, sanitation, and basic healthcare, with children under the age of three especially vulnerable to infection. The infection causes severe inflammation on the inside of the […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced a protracted humanitarian crisis exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, disrupting healthcare services and access to clean water in rural areas. As the country seeks to rebuild its international image and foster regional ties, participation in events like this marathon could enhance Ethiopia's presence in East African sports and promote unity amidst ongoing political challenges.

articles.title: TPLF Rejects EU Criticism, Accuses Federal Government of Violating Peace Deal
articles.summary: The Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) has rejected European Union accusations that it is undermining Ethiopia’s 2022 peace agreement, instead accusing the federal government of violating the accord and warning that the Tigray region faces a growing risk of renewed conflict. In a statement issued in response to the European Union delegation’s call for the […]
articles.context: The TPLF and the Ethiopian federal government signed a peace agreement on November 2, 2022, aimed at ending a brutal two-year conflict in Tigray that resulted in significant humanitarian crises. Tensions have resurfaced as both sides accuse each other of failing to uphold the terms of the agreement, particularly regarding disarmament and humanitarian access, raising concerns about the stability of the region and the potential for renewed violence.

articles.title: Resumption of Talks on Tigray is Urgent, Says EU

We fully support the mediation efforts led by the AU’s High Representative President Obasanjo to achieve peace and stability,” said the EU, […]
articles.context: The Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), has resulted in a humanitarian crisis and widespread violence. The signing of the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022 aimed to cease hostilities, but implementation has faced challenges. The EU's call for renewed talks highlights the urgency of addressing ongoing tensions and ensuring compliance with the agreement to foster lasting peace in the region.

articles.title: Ugandan ICC Judge Solomy Bossa Takes On Trump Administration Over Controversial Sanctions
articles.summary: International Criminal Court (ICC) Judge Solomy Balungi Bossa has joined a landmark legal challenge against the administration of The post Ugandan ICC Judge Solomy Bossa Takes On Trump Administration Over Controversial Sanctions appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: Judge Solomy Balungi Bossa's challenge comes amid heightened tensions between the International Criminal Court and the Trump administration, which imposed sanctions on ICC officials in 2020, including Bossa, in response to investigations into U.S. actions in Afghanistan. articles.title: How Editing Alongside CGI Enhances the Magical Feel in Movies
articles.summary: Movies have always been a gateway to worlds beyond our imagination. Whether audiences are watching dragons soar through the skies, enchanted forests come alive, or superheroes wield extraordinary powers, the sense of wonder comes from more than just visual effects. While computer-generated imagery (CGI) often receives the spotlight, film editing plays an equally important role […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the film industry is experiencing a renaissance, fueled by a growing interest in storytelling and visual arts. The 2020 establishment of the Ethiopian Film Industry Development Council aims to promote local filmmakers and enhance production quality. Amid ongoing political tensions and conflict in regions like Tigray, the rise of CGI and advanced editing techniques in Ethiopian cinema offers a means for cultural expression and unity, reflecting the country's diverse narratives and aspirations.

articles.title: U.S. furniture Giant Ashley Opens Second Ethiopian Branch
articles.summary: Yusra Home, the exclusive distributor of Ashley Furniture in Ethiopia, has officially opened its second showroom in the country. This move is stated to further strengthen the American furniture giant’s footprint in the East African market. The new branch, opened in the Gourd Shola area of Addis Ababa, follows the successful opening of the first […]
articles.context: The opening of Ashley Furniture's second showroom in Ethiopia comes amid a recovering economy following the Tigray conflict, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022. The Ethiopian government is actively seeking foreign investment to bolster economic growth and stability, as outlined in its Homegrown Economic Reform agenda launched in 2020. This expansion by a major U.S. retailer signals renewed confidence in the Ethiopian market and its potential for international business.

articles.title: Ethiopian Cargo Lands 2026 AFLAS Award for Best African Air Cargo Carrier
articles.summary: Ethiopian Cargo and Logistics Services, Africa’s largest air cargo network, has won the 2026 Asia Freight, Logistics and Supply Chain (AFLAS) Award for Best Air Cargo Carrier –
articles.context: Ethiopian Cargo and Logistics Services has emerged as a key player in the region's logistics sector, particularly amid the ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy and enhance its global trade connections, this award highlights the country's efforts to position itself as a logistics hub in East Africa, especially in light of increasing competition from neighboring countries.

articles.title: Strengthening Families: Lessons from Decades of Alternative Care in Ethiopia
articles.summary: By Martha Alemayehu* Ethiopia is home to one of the youngest and fastest-growing child populations in the world. With nearly 40 percent of the population being children under
articles.context: Ethiopia's ongoing internal conflicts, particularly the Tigray War that erupted in November 2020, have exacerbated vulnerabilities among children, leading to increased reliance on alternative care systems. The Ethiopian government, alongside NGOs like Save the Children, has been working to reform child welfare policies to address the needs of displaced and orphaned children. This context highlights the urgent need for effective family-based care solutions amidst a backdrop of social upheaval and humanitarian crises.

articles.summary: Shock has gripped Rubirizi and Bushenyi districts after the death of a Senior Three student, Ankunda Sheila, at The post TRAGEDY IN RUIRIZI! Senior Three Student Dies After Alleged Teacher Beating as School Is Closed appeared first on The Nile Post .
articles.context: The incident occurs against a backdrop of heightened tensions in Uganda's education system, where allegations of corporal punishment have sparked national debates on child rights and teacher accountability. This incident also highlights ongoing societal struggles with violence in schools, particularly in regions like Rubirizi, where community trust in educational institutions is increasingly fragile.

articles.title: Muhoozi’s Remarks on Lukwago Spark Political Firestorm Online
articles.summary: A fresh wave of political debate has erupted in Uganda after controversial social media remarks attributed to Muhoozi The post Muhoozi’s Remarks on Lukwago Spark Political Firestorm Online appeared first on The Nile Post .

articles.title: Taking Cover
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with the aftermath of the Tigray War, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. However, tensions persist in regions like Oromia and Amhara, where ethnic conflicts and government crackdowns continue to escalate. The ongoing humanitarian crisis, exacerbated by conflict and displacement, highlights the urgent need for stability and reconciliation as the country approaches national elections scheduled for 2025.

articles.title: Remembering History
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently navigating a complex political landscape following the Tigray War, which officially ended with a peace agreement on November 2, 2022. The conflict, rooted in ethnic tensions and power struggles, has left deep scars and ongoing humanitarian challenges. As the country seeks to rebuild and reconcile, remembering its historical narratives becomes crucial in addressing grievances and fostering unity among diverse ethnic groups, particularly the Tigrayans and the Amhara.

articles.title: Chilling
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently grappling with the aftermath of the Tigray War, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022, yet tensions remain high in various regions, particularly in Oromia and Amhara. The ongoing conflict has exacerbated ethnic divisions and humanitarian crises, with reports of violence and displacement continuing to surface. Additionally, the government's efforts to consolidate power amid these tensions complicate the political landscape, making the situation increasingly precarious.

articles.title: Urgent Action Needed to Ease Engine MRO Bottlenecks
articles.summary: The International Air Transport Association (IATA), in collaboration with Emerton, today released a new study examining bottlenecks in the maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) of latest-generation single-aisle aircraft engines—namely LEAP engines from CFM, and Geared Turbofan (GTF) engines from Pratt & Whitney. The study, Single Aisle Aircraft Engines MRO: Strategic Levers to Address Supply Chain […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's aviation sector has faced significant challenges due to the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which has disrupted supply chains and maintenance operations. The Ethiopian Airlines fleet, heavily reliant on LEAP and GTF engines, is crucial for regional connectivity and economic recovery. The recent peace agreement in November 2022 has created a renewed urgency to address MRO bottlenecks to support the country's rebuilding efforts and enhance its role as a key aviation hub in East Africa.

articles.title: Is Ethiopia’s Credit Cap Doing More Harm Than Good?
articles.summary: By AyeleGelan (PhD) For more than two years, Ethiopia’s economic policy has revolved around one overriding objective: bringing inflation under control. Few would dispute that this goal is important. Double-digit inflation has eroded household purchasing power, discouraged investment and complicated business planning. Yet an equally important question deserves attention: has the cure become more damaging […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, which disrupted supply chains and increased inflation. The government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, implemented a credit cap in 2021 to stabilize the economy, but critics argue it may hinder growth and investment. As the country seeks to recover from the conflict and economic turmoil, the effectiveness of this policy is under intense scrutiny.

articles.title: Diverging Assessments Delay Ethiopia’s Debt Restructuring Prospects
articles.summary: “Ethiopia is left to navigate between hardening positions on both sides”: MoF Advisor Inconsistencies between IMF and creditor assessments of the Ethiopian economy is proving a hurdle in Ethiopia’s efforts to secure debt treatment under the G20 Common Framework, according to the Paris Club’s 2025 report. The report highlights the agreement-in-principle reached in March 2025 […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's debt restructuring efforts are complicated by ongoing tensions stemming from the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020 and has strained the country's economy. The IMF's differing assessments of Ethiopia's economic stability, contrasted with creditor expectations, reflect the broader geopolitical dynamics at play, particularly as the G20 Common Framework aims to address debt vulnerabilities for low-income countries. The Paris Club's 2025 report underscores the urgency of resolving these discrepancies for Ethiopia's financial recovery.

articles.title: Eritrea’s “Low-Cost” Destabilization Strategy Poses Serious Security Threat: Getachew Reda
articles.summary: US embassy urges dialogue in Tigray Eritrea’s primary security threat to Ethiopia no longer lies in conventional warfare but in organizing, financing, and coordinating armed groups operating inside the country, says Getachew Reda, advisor to the Prime Minister and former leader of the Tigray Interim Administration. Speaking at the Annual National Security Conference of the […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia's Tigray region, which erupted in November 2020, has seen Eritrea actively supporting Ethiopian federal forces against Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) fighters. Despite a peace agreement signed in November 2022, tensions remain high, with Eritrea's involvement in destabilizing activities within Ethiopia raising alarms. Getachew Reda's comments highlight concerns over Eritrea's shift from conventional military engagement to a strategy of supporting insurgent groups, complicating the fragile peace.

articles.title: Airbus Analysis Spotlights ET African Market Growth, Identifies Lucrative Routes
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines is making strides in capturing market share from Gulf and regional competitors in routes connecting African hubs to centers like London and Dubai, according to an analysis published by European aerospace giant Airbus. The report, dubbed ‘Exploring the Horizons: A Study of Unserved Air Routes to, from and within Sub-Saharan Africa’ and published […]
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines has historically been a key player in African aviation, but recent geopolitical tensions, including the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, have reshaped regional dynamics. As the airline seeks to expand its market share, it faces competition from Gulf carriers like Emirates and Qatar Airways, which have dominated intercontinental routes. The ongoing recovery from the pandemic further complicates the landscape, making Airbus's insights particularly timely for Ethiopian Airlines' strategic planning.

articles.title: Afreximbank Urges Focus on Intra-African Trade, Showcases Rising Payment System Adoption
articles.summary: East African countries are accelerating the adoption of local currency settlement for cross border trade through the Pan-African Payment and Settlement System (PAPSS) as the continent pushes to reduce dependence on the US dollar and euro, according to the African Trade Report 2026. The report published by Afreximbank identifies digital payment infrastructure as a central […]
articles.context: The push for intra-African trade through local currency settlements comes amid ongoing tensions in the Horn of Africa, particularly between Ethiopia and its neighbors following the Tigray conflict, which ended in November 2022. The African Union's Agenda 2063 emphasizes economic integration, and the adoption of the Pan-African Payment and Settlement System (PAPSS) is crucial for enhancing trade relationships, especially as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy and strengthen regional ties post-conflict.

articles.title: The National Dialogue’s Defining Test: Independence, Inclusion, and Trust
articles.summary: On June 24, the Ethiopian National Dialogue Commission (ENDC) marked its most significant milestone to date by officially unveiling eight core thematic agenda pillars. Ranging from state-building, the structure and form of government, and the status of federal cities, to peacebuilding, transitional justice, and human rights these pillars are intended to structurally anchor the highly […]
articles.context: The Ethiopian National Dialogue Commission was established in 2021 amid escalating ethnic tensions and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that began in November 2020. The dialogue aims to address deep-rooted grievances and foster national unity among diverse ethnic groups, especially after the 2022 peace agreement between the federal government and Tigray forces. The unveiling of the eight thematic pillars on June 24, 2023, is a critical step towards achieving lasting peace and stability in Ethiopia.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s Megaproject Push Leaves Legacy of Debt and Overexpansion, Officials Concede
articles.summary: Ethiopian officials have acknowledged that decades of state-led megaproject expansion left the country burdened with massive debt and underutilized infrastructure, prompting a shift toward a more commercially driven approach to public investment. Speaking on Ethiopia’s infrastructure and investment trajectory on the Ethiopia Delivers Summit this week, Ethiopian Investment Holdings (EIH) chief Brook Taye (PhD) said […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's ambitious infrastructure projects, particularly since the launch of the Growth and Transformation Plan in 2010, have led to an estimated debt exceeding $30 billion. The ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which erupted in November 2020, has strained resources and diverted attention from economic development. The recent shift in investment strategy reflects a response to these challenges, as the government seeks to stabilize the economy and attract foreign investment amid ongoing regional tensions.

articles.title: Housing Market Study Set 11pct Benchmark for Annual Rent Increase
articles.summary: A residential rental market study commissioned by the Addis Ababa City Administration has concluded that maintaining annual consumer inflation below 10 percent could stabilize natural rent increases at 11.24 percent, providing what researchers describe as a key benchmark for managing the city’s rental market while reducing the need for aggressive state rent controls. The findings […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with inflation and economic instability, particularly following the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, which disrupted supply chains and increased living costs. The Addis Ababa City Administration's study comes at a crucial time as the government seeks to balance economic recovery with housing affordability, especially amidst rising urban migration and a growing population in the capital. The proposed benchmark aims to mitigate tensions over housing while avoiding stringent rent controls.

articles.title: Integrating Refugees: Risk or Opportunity?
articles.summary: The burden of sheltering the multitudes fleeing conflict, insecurity, persecution, and climate shocks from across the Horn of Africa and beyond has long fallen upon Ethiopia. articles.context: Ethiopia has been a key host for refugees due to its geographical location and historical ties with neighboring countries experiencing conflict, such as South Sudan and Somalia. The Tigray conflict, which escalated in November 2020, has further intensified the refugee crisis, with many fleeing violence and instability. Additionally, the 2021 peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and Tigray forces has created a complex political landscape, impacting both internal displacement and refugee integration efforts.

articles.title: Seismic Shifts in Global Politics Beckon New Chapter for Ethiopia-Canada Ties
articles.summary: Nicolas Simard is Canada’s ambassador to Ethiopia and Djibouti, as well as Head of Mission for Sudan. Before assuming his current role in July 2025, Simard served as ambassador to Mali, DRC, Nigeria, Bangladesh, in addition to a stint as director of planning and evaluation at the International Organization of La Francophonie, in Paris. His […]
articles.context: The recent appointment of Nicolas Simard as Canada’s ambassador to Ethiopia comes at a critical juncture following the signing of the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022, which aimed to end the Tigray conflict. As Ethiopia navigates post-conflict recovery and regional tensions with Sudan over border disputes, Canada’s renewed diplomatic engagement could play a pivotal role in fostering stability and addressing humanitarian needs in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: “Fundamentally Different”: AASTU President on Higher Learning, Industry 4.0

Dereje Engida is a Chartered Engineer […]

The government's focus on technological advancement and higher education, as emphasized by AASTU President Dereje Engida, is part of a broader strategy to foster national unity and economic recovery. This shift is crucial as Ethiopia seeks to rebuild and modernize its economy amid ongoing ethnic tensions and regional instability.

articles.title: حتى الشباب والأصحّاء يُنصحون بتجنب الجري والإفراط في شرب الكحول خلال موجات الحر
articles.summary: وسط موجة حار تشهدها المملكة المتحدة، يحذر الخبراء من تزايد حالات توقف القلب خلال فترات الطقس شديد الحرارة، مشيرين إلى أن تأثير الطقس الحار لن يؤثر فقط على كبار السن وذوي البنية الضعيفة فحسب، بل من الممكن أن يطال الأصحاء وأصحاب اللياقة البدنية.
articles.context: تأتي هذه التحذيرات في وقت تعاني فيه منطقة القرن الأفريقي، بما في ذلك الصومال، من آثار تغير المناخ، مما يزيد من حدة موجات الحر. في عام 2022، شهدت الصومال أسوأ موجة جفاف منذ عقود، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأزمات الصحية. مع تزايد الضغوط على النظام الصحي، يصبح من الضروري توعية الناس حول المخاطر الصحية المرتبطة بالحرارة، خاصة في ظل عدم الاستقرار السياسي والاقتصادي الذي تشهده البلاد.

The Ethiopian Electric Utility's partnership with Alpha TND and Capital Electech, backed by World Bank funding, is crucial for restoring stability and improving energy access in the affected areas, aligning with the government's broader efforts to rebuild and foster economic recovery post-conflict.

articles.title: Taza Legal Moves to End Ethiopia’s Reliance on International Firms

Historically, complex […]

The merger of Taza Legal LLP comes at a time when the Ethiopian government is emphasizing national sovereignty and local capacity building, especially in light of ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and the need for legal expertise to navigate complex issues arising from the conflict and economic recovery efforts.

articles.title: Spiro closes $270 million round with new $55 million backing from NewTrails Capital

articles.context: Spiro's funding comes at a crucial time as Ethiopia and neighboring countries are increasingly prioritizing sustainable energy solutions amid ongoing economic challenges and climate change impacts. The African Union's Agenda 2063 emphasizes the need for infrastructure development, and the recent peace agreement in Ethiopia (November 2022) has opened avenues for investment in green technologies. This funding could enhance regional cooperation in electric mobility, aligning with Ethiopia's ambitions to become a leader in renewable energy.

articles.title: Beyond Discounts: Building a Sustainable Electronic Taxi Industry

articles.context: Ethiopia's electronic taxi industry has emerged against a backdrop of rapid urbanization and economic reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018. The government's push for digitalization and foreign investment, coupled with the ongoing Tigray conflict and its impact on infrastructure, has created both challenges and opportunities for the sector. The recent influx of new ride-hailing apps, such as ZayRide and Ride, highlights the competitive landscape as the country seeks to stabilize and grow its economy.

articles.title: Over 1,400 displaced in Sudan's North Darfur in single day: UN
articles.summary: Fresh displacement from 5 villages in North Darfur comes days after another wave of forced movement as insecurity continues to escalate
articles.context: The recent displacement in North Darfur is part of a broader crisis stemming from the conflict that erupted in 2003, involving the Sudanese government and various rebel groups, notably the Sudan Liberation Army (SLA) and the Justice and Equality Movement (JEM). The 2021 military coup exacerbated instability, undermining the fragile peace agreements like the Juba Agreement of 2020. The United Nations has reported a surge in violence, leading to increased humanitarian needs and displacement in the region.

articles.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
articles.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.
articles.context: The protests in Kenya stem from widespread discontent over the government's handling of economic issues and alleged electoral fraud during the 2022 presidential elections, which saw William Ruto assume power amid accusations of irregularities. The 2024 demonstrations, marked by violent clashes with security forces, resulted in the deaths of dozens, prompting ongoing calls for accountability from human rights organizations and opposition leaders like Raila Odinga, who continue to challenge the government's legitimacy.

articles.title: Kenyans mark two years since Gen Z protests
articles.summary: Two years on, families of those killed during Kenya’s 2024 anti-government protests returned to Parliament to remember their loved ones and demand justice.
articles.context: In 2024, Kenya experienced widespread anti-government protests sparked by allegations of electoral fraud and economic mismanagement under President William Ruto's administration. The protests, which were largely led by youth groups, resulted in violent clashes with security forces, leaving several demonstrators dead. As the country approaches the 2026 elections, the unresolved issues surrounding these protests and the demand for accountability from the government remain critical to the political landscape and public sentiment in Kenya.

articles.title: Families honor victims as Kenya marks anniversary of deadly youth-led protests
articles.summary: Commemorations coincide with fresh protests, arrests in Nairobi
articles.context: The anniversary of the youth-led protests in Kenya, which erupted in 2020 following the police killing of George Floyd-inspired demonstrations, highlights ongoing tensions between the government and civil society. The protests have been fueled by widespread discontent over police brutality and economic hardships exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic. Recent arrests during commemorations in Nairobi reflect the government's continued crackdown on dissent, raising concerns about human rights and political freedoms ahead of the 2024 elections.

articles.title: طلاب سودانيون يعودون إلى معهد النور للمكفوفين برغم الدمار

articles.title: Authority Registers Bunna Bank Shares, Approves New Offering

articles.title: 215 civilians die in RSF-run prison in South Darfur: Sudanese medics

articles.title: WHO warns about worsening humanitarian crisis, cholera outbreak in Sudan's Kordofan region
articles.summary: More than 700 suspected cases, 105 deaths reported in West Kordofan since mid-May, according to authorities
articles.context: The Kordofan region of Sudan has been plagued by conflict since the Darfur War began in 2003, with ongoing violence between government forces and various armed groups, including the Sudan People's Liberation Movement-North. The political instability intensified following the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019, leading to a power vacuum and worsening humanitarian conditions. The recent cholera outbreak compounds the crisis, as access to healthcare and clean water remains severely limited amidst ongoing clashes.

articles.title: Central Bank’s FX Auction Sees Average rate Settles at 157 Birr per Dollar
articles.summary: The average rate of Ethiopian Birr (ETB) has slightly strengthened against the US dollar in the foreign exchange trading on Tuesday, the results released by the Central Bank

articles.title: Explainer: Critical constraints risk further Ebola spread as cases surpass 1,000
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – As the Bundibugyo Ebola virus outbreak continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, with cases already surpassing 1,000 and
articles.context: The ongoing Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda is exacerbated by the region's fragile healthcare systems, which have been strained by years of conflict and political instability. articles.context: South Sudan gained independence from Sudan in 2011 but has faced ongoing civil conflict since 2013, leading to a humanitarian crisis and political instability. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish peace and a transitional government, paving the way for these elections. The December 22 vote is crucial for consolidating peace and restoring public trust in governance after years of turmoil and leadership disputes.
